name: HexStrike AI
what_it_does: MCP server that enables AI agents to autonomously execute 150+ cybersecurity tools across network recon, web app testing, auth cracking, binary analysis, cloud security, and CTF/forensics. Features 12+ specialized AI agents for orchestrating complex security workflows.
use_cases: Automated penetration testing with AI-driven tool selection
Bug bounty hunting with autonomous vulnerability discovery
CTF challenge solving with multi-tool orchestration
not_for: Replacing manual expert review of critical findings
best_when: You need an AI agent to orchestrate complex multi-tool security assessments and want autonomous tool selection based on discovered targets.
avoid_when: You need simple single-tool scanning, are not authorized to test the target, or require compliance-certified tooling with audit trails.

auth.notes: No authentication required for the MCP server itself. Individual security tools may require their own API keys or credentials.

pricing.notes: MIT license. Free and open source. Underlying tools (nmap, sqlmap, etc.) are also free.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Graceful degradation documented but error messages from 150+ tools vary widely in quality.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Security scans are inherently non-idempotent; repeated runs may produce different results and can trigger target-side defenses.
known_agent_gotchas: Requires 150+ security tools installed on the host system
Chrome/Chromium required for browser agent functionality
Security testing without authorization is illegal
Long-running scans may exceed MCP client timeouts
Default 300-second timeout may not be enough for complex scans

security_score_components.security_notes: Community/specialized tool. Apply standard security practices for category. Review documentation for specific security requirements.
